VNET.headline: What's Going On With VNET Stock On Wednesday?
VNET.description: VNET Group shares surged 28.44% on Wednesday following news that PJ Millennium I and II Limited agreed to acquire nearly 650 million shares at $1.44 per share ($8.69 per ADS), giving them approximately 38.1% ownership. The deal is expected to close in Q4 2026 pending shareholder approval. Despite positive company-specific news, broader markets showed mixed performance with the Russell 2000 down 0.33% and S&P 500 up 0.04%.

TSEM.headline: Why Tower Semiconductor Stock Climbed Today
TSEM.description: Tower Semiconductor shares surged 18% after issuing an upbeat growth forecast driven by strong demand for its silicon photonics technology used in AI infrastructure. The company reported 62% net profit growth to $65 million with expanding margins, projects 22% revenue growth to $455 million in Q2, and secured $1.3 billion in photonics contracts for 2027. Management reaffirmed its 2028 targets of $2.8 billion in revenue and $750 million in net profit.

AAOI.headline: SanDisk And Micron Had Their Run – This New 'Photonics' ETF Has 15 Triple-Digit Gainers Inside
AAOI.description: A new photonics ETF (EUV) launched on May 6, 2026, focusing on optical interconnect technology for AI data centers. The fund holds 15 stocks already up over 100% year-to-date, driven by structural demand from hyperscaler capex and supply constraints in indium phosphide lasers. However, critics note the ETF's top holdings lean toward general semiconductor names rather than pure photonics plays, duplicating exposure available in broader semiconductor funds.

WOLF.headline: Navitas Breaks Out on India Deal, Validating High-Power AI Pivot
WOLF.description: Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) surged 24% on May 11 following a strategic partnership with Cyient to establish gallium nitride (GaN) manufacturing in India. The move validates the company's pivot from low-margin mobile chargers to high-growth AI data centers and EV infrastructure. With strong sequential revenue growth (18% Q4-to-Q1), a robust balance sheet ($221M cash, no debt), and dual GaN/SiC technology capabilities, Navitas is positioned as a key supplier for NVIDIA's next-generation AI platfo

NBIS.headline: Shelter and Gasoline Pushed CPI to 3.8%. Producer Prices Are Running at 6%. Here Is What That Pipeline of Inflation Means for Rate Cuts and Your Portfolio
NBIS.description: The April Producer Price Index rose 6% year-over-year, significantly exceeding expectations of 4.6%, signaling potential future consumer price increases. Combined with the previous day's CPI report, this inflation data has reduced expectations for interest rate cuts in 2026, with markets now pricing in a potential rate hike. Tech stocks rebounded on strong earnings from neocloud companies and optimism around chip sales, but inflation-sensitive sectors like financials and retail remain under pres

AKAM.headline: Why Are Akamai Shares Trading Higher On Wednesday?
AKAM.description: Akamai Technologies shares rose 4.95% following strong Q1 earnings that beat analyst expectations and a BofA Securities upgrade from Neutral to Buy with a $175 price target. The analyst highlighted the company's transformation into an AI infrastructure platform, driven by a major $1.8 billion seven-year contract and 40% year-over-year growth in Cloud Infrastructure Services. The company projects revenue growth to accelerate from 7% in 2026 to 11% in 2027.

RKLB.headline: SpaceX IPO Could Put Weak Space Stocks 'Into Obscurity,' Rocket Lab CFO Says
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab's CFO Adam Spice warns that a potential SpaceX IPO in mid-2026 could create a market divide in space stocks, where high-quality companies with real infrastructure and recurring contracts benefit while weaker players face obscurity. SpaceX's entry into public markets would establish a clear benchmark for the sector, forcing investors to discriminate between legitimate space operators and companies relying on promotional narratives.

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Just Hit a New All-Time High—Time to Buy or Let It Breathe?
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab (RKLB) surged over 40% to an all-time high of $117.35 following strong Q1 2026 earnings with record $200.3M revenue and a $2.2B backlog. The company announced major contracts including its largest-ever launch deal, a $30M hypersonic contract, and the acquisition of Motiv Space Systems. While analysts raised price targets to $120 (Street-high), the stock appears overbought with RSI in the 70s and trading 20% above consensus targets, suggesting consolidation may be prudent before furthe

RKLB.headline: Stock Market Today, May 11: Markets Inch Upwards Even As Oil Prices Rise
RKLB.description: U.S. stock markets rose modestly on May 11, 2026, with tech and AI stocks driving gains despite rising oil prices and inflation concerns. The S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow all gained around 0.2%, fueled by semiconductor and AI rallies. However, surging crude oil prices near $99/barrel pressured airlines and rekindled inflation worries, while investment banks predict the Federal Reserve may delay rate cuts until 2026.

POET.headline: POET CLASS ACTION ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Announces that a Class Action Lawsuit Has Been Filed Against POET Technologies, Inc. and Encourages Investors to Contact the Firm
POET.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against POET Technologies, Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding its tax status as a passive foreign investment company (PFIC), which could negatively impact U.S. stockholders. The complaint also alleges that an executive violated a non-disclosure agreement by discussing business agreements publicly. Investors who purchased POET securities between April 1-27, 2026 can join the lawsuit by June 29, 2026.

FLNC.headline: Fluence Announces Pricing of Secondary Offering of Class A Common Stock by Existing Controlling Stockholders
FLNC.description: Fluence Energy announced the pricing of a secondary offering of 20 million shares of Class A common stock by existing controlling stockholders at $21.00 per share, with an additional 3 million share option for underwriters. The company will not receive proceeds from this offering, which is expected to close on May 15, 2026. Major investment banks including Barclays, Goldman Sachs, and J.P. Morgan are managing the offering.

LITE.headline: The AI Supercycle Has Moved Beyond Memory Chips. This Supercharged Tech Stock Has Gained 169% in 2026 and Is Riding the Next Wave
LITE.description: Lumentum Holdings has surged 169% in 2026 as demand for optical and photonics components critical to AI data centers far outpaces supply. The company reported 90% revenue growth and 4.1x earnings growth in Q3 2026, with CEO noting supply-demand imbalance exceeding 30%. With optical networking market expected to grow 39% annually to $73 billion by 2030 and component shortages persisting through 2027, analysts project continued strong earnings growth, though the stock trades at a premium 159x curr

LITE.headline: SanDisk And Micron Had Their Run – This New 'Photonics' ETF Has 15 Triple-Digit Gainers Inside
LITE.description: A new photonics ETF (EUV) launched on May 6, 2026, focusing on optical interconnect technology for AI data centers. The fund holds 15 stocks already up over 100% year-to-date, driven by structural demand from hyperscaler capex and supply constraints in indium phosphide lasers. However, critics note the ETF's top holdings lean toward general semiconductor names rather than pure photonics plays, duplicating exposure available in broader semiconductor funds.

RAL.headline: Nasdaq 100 Dips On Hot CPI, Sandisk And Micron Tumble 9%: Stock Market Today
RAL.description: U.S. equities fell sharply on May 12, 2026, after April's CPI came in hotter than expected at 3.8%, raising concerns the Federal Reserve will keep rates on hold throughout the year. The Nasdaq 100 sank 2.1% as the semiconductor sector collapsed, with memory chip stocks hit particularly hard by South Korea's proposed 'AI Citizen Dividend.' Notable gainers included Zebra Technologies (+18%) and Wendy's (+15%), while ZoomInfo plummeted 33% on slashed guidance.

WEN.headline: A Real Biggie Deal: Wendy’s Serves Up a Juicy Buyout Arbitrage
WEN.description: Activist investor Nelson Peltz's Trian Fund Management is exploring a take-private bid for Wendy's, sending shares up nearly 15%. The move capitalizes on the company's compressed valuation (P/E of ~10), high short interest (29.32% of float), and ongoing turnaround efforts through Project Fresh, which includes restaurant closures, menu revitalization with 'Biggie Deals,' and international expansion in China. The 7% dividend yield provides downside protection while investors await M&A and operatio

WEN.headline: Nasdaq 100 Dips On Hot CPI, Sandisk And Micron Tumble 9%: Stock Market Today
WEN.description: U.S. equities fell sharply on May 12, 2026, after April's CPI came in hotter than expected at 3.8%, raising concerns the Federal Reserve will keep rates on hold throughout the year. The Nasdaq 100 sank 2.1% as the semiconductor sector collapsed, with memory chip stocks hit particularly hard by South Korea's proposed 'AI Citizen Dividend.' Notable gainers included Zebra Technologies (+18%) and Wendy's (+15%), while ZoomInfo plummeted 33% on slashed guidance.

WEN.headline: Why Is Wendy's Stock Surging Over 14% In Tuesday Pre-Market?
WEN.description: Wendy's stock surged 14% in pre-market trading after reports that Nelson Peltz's Trian Fund Management is seeking financing for a potential takeover bid to privatize the fast-food chain. However, no formal acquisition approach has been made. The surge comes despite Wendy's facing significant challenges, including a 45% stock decline over the past year, shrinking U.S. store count, declining sales traffic, and a JPMorgan downgrade to 'Underweight' with a price target cut to $6.

DDOG.headline: 1 Glorious Growth Stock to Buy Hand Over Fist in May, According to Wall Street
DDOG.description: Datadog's AI-focused observability products are experiencing explosive demand, with the company reporting accelerated revenue growth to $1 billion in Q1 2026 (32% YoY increase) and raising full-year guidance. The stock has surged 40% since earnings and hit record highs. Wall Street analysts overwhelmingly recommend buying, with an average price target of $223.30 suggesting 10% upside, though the stock trades at a premium valuation (P/S ratio of 20) compared to peers.

CRCL.headline: Circle IPO Math Looks Richer After Arc Draws BlackRock and A16z
CRCL.description: Circle's stock surged 15% after securing $222M in presale funding for Arc, its new Layer-1 blockchain for institutional stablecoin settlement, valued at $3B. The round attracted major institutional investors including BlackRock, Andreessen Horowitz, and others. Circle's corporate valuation is anticipated around $4B-$5B ahead of its IPO. BlackRock's BUIDL fund, which has grown to $3.2B in AUM, relies on Circle's USDC smart contract for 24/7 redemptions, with Circle generating $150M in protocol fe

CRCL.headline: Is Circle Internet Group Stock Due for a Big Rally After This Positive Crypto News?
CRCL.description: Circle Internet Group's stock rallied 15.91% following positive developments around the Clarity Act, which will allow stablecoin rewards for non-passive activities. The compromise eases concerns about a complete ban on stablecoin yields. Circle's USDC stablecoin saw 28% year-over-year growth and revenue rose 20% to $694 million. However, the stock remains down 56% from its 52-week high, and analysts recommend cautious positioning given ongoing regulatory uncertainty.

CRCL.headline: My Top 2 Recent Tech IPOs to Buy for May 2026
CRCL.description: Circle and CoreWeave are highlighted as two strong tech IPOs worth buying in May 2026. Circle, which went public in June 2025, has quadrupled from its $31 IPO price and issues USD Coin stablecoin with growing reserve interest income. CoreWeave, which went public in March 2025, has nearly tripled from its $40 IPO price and operates 43 AI data centers with 250,000+ GPUs, showing massive growth potential with a $99.4 billion backlog.
